headline: The Highway Contracts You Are Not Allowed to Read
context: Works Ministry confirmed that 21 toll concession agreements remain classified under the Official Secrets Act despite repeated parliamentary questions since 2008.

cards.big: You pay the toll. You cannot read the contract.
cards.sub: Twenty-one concession agreements. All classified. Exposed clauses show guaranteed traffic volumes.

cards.big: Leaked clauses reveal government compensation if traffic falls below targets.
cards.sub: Taxpayers pay toll companies when people drive less. The incentive is perverse.

cards.big: Toll revenue: RM5.1B in 2025. Government compensation: RM1.2B on top.
cards.sub: The public pays twice — at the booth and through taxes.

cards.big: PH promised declassification in 2018. Retracted within 6 months.
cards.sub: Every government that enters office discovers why secrecy is convenient.

cards.big: What kind of contract requires secrecy from the people paying for it?
cards.sub: Classification protects terms, not security. Ask whose interests that serves.

cards.big: A contract between government and a private company, paid by the public, classified from the public. The structure answers its own question.
